channel.title: Comments for page "The Streamliner"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Streamliner"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like parts of this, but I can't quite upvote it. I agree with Mighty that the build-up to the Darkblade confrontation isn't very interesting, and I would like to see more of Isabel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually… after reading this I'm seriously considering rewriting the ending. Thanks for taking a look!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the Streamliner. This piece has moments of brilliance, such as the exchange between SCP-4455 and SCP-777-J, but it has two large issues that make me hesitant to upvote.

First is more minor. SCP-4455 is described as being a reality-bender, rather than just describing what happens. Part of the joy in reading the scip is realizing what exactly his "power" is, and enjoying the flow around the narrative holes he creates.

Second is more major. The ending basically lampshades everything in a way I dislike. A lot of the impact of the Streamliner comes from imagining a person actually behaving this way, where somehow they can "bypass local lines of causality" by just performing an action and that's it. Especially the end, where credits roll, somehow, in real life. It's like the ultimate "narrative-bending" thing you can do, but unfortunately it all goes away if it literally is just fiction rather than in-universe. The dialogue within Wondertainment doesn't really do anything for me either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That part was meant to be ridiculous, but I see what you're saying. I think I could have made it more clear it was parody.

Thanks for reading though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While this is a substantial improvement over the version that I critted in chat, I still can't say I'm sold on this. The fight with Darkblade was fantastic, but the buildup still felt like a slog, and I still rolled my eyes hard at the "I'll turn my life around" bit. Overall, this is a novote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Think of it this way…" It was going to be a long meating.

I think that meatings are more of a Sarkic thing. Though now I want to see what happens when Wondertainment tries to cater to that crowd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's an extremely belated Jamcon entry using every theme at once that I've been working on since February. Just to be clear the Streamliner 'really' did defeat Dark Blade. Isabel based her film on a true story. Anyway:

Special Thanks to:

TheMightyMcB, Zyn, and DrChandra for helping me work on my many drafts for this.

Extra Special Thanks to DarkStuff for helping me with this and writing SCP-4455 which is awesome and you need to read if you haven't already.
